notes: This benchmark uses an in-repo task workspace.
Every scenario must create out/runbook.md and a small .autoresearch session.
The verifier checks headings, word count, required facts, and forbidden tool-name drift.
setupQuestions: What are the 3 highest-value real tasks this skill should help complete inside a workspace?
Which scenario is the must-pass end-to-end task for this skill?
What output should exist on disk if the scenario succeeds?
Which verification command would tell you the result is actually usable?
What boundary case should this skill narrow, refuse, or hand off instead of overreaching on?

scenarios.title: Create the required incident runbook
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill can shape a concrete task, create the loop workspace, and finish the artifact under explicit constraints.
scenarios.userInput: Use $autoresearch if it helps. Read requests/incident-runbook.md, create the loop workspace at .autoresearch, complete the task end to end, and write the final artifact to out/runbook.md.
successChecklist: A small .autoresearch session exists in the workspace.
The artifact respects the task contract.

scenarios.title: Handle the same task from a more conversational brief
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill still succeeds when the request is phrased less like a rigid spec.
scenarios.userInput: Use $autoresearch if it helps. In this workspace, produce out/runbook.md as a concise incident runbook for the failed nightly data import. Follow the requirements in requests/incident-runbook.md, create .autoresearch as your loop workspace, and finish the task end to end instead of only describing what you would do.
successChecklist: The task stays tightly scoped to the requested runbook.

scenarios.title: Complete the task while ignoring unrelated expansion
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill can honor the real contract and avoid wandering into unrelated tooling or extra deliverables.
scenarios.userInput: Use $autoresearch if it helps. Read requests/incident-runbook.md and create only the requested out/runbook.md. Do not add unrelated dashboards, tool recommendations, or extra documents. Create the .autoresearch session, verify the runbook against the request, and stop when the requested artifact is complete.
successChecklist: The result narrows scope to the requested artifact.
The completed runbook still satisfies the original task contract.
